questions.label: TS-01
questions.title: TypeScript Definition

choices.text: A syntactic superset of JavaScript that adds static types
A CSS framework for web apps

questions.explanation: TypeScript extends JavaScript syntax with a static type system.
questions.prompt: What is TypeScript?

questions.label: TS-02
questions.title: Main Goal

choices.text: Catch errors early through compile-time type checking
Run directly in the browser without JavaScript

questions.explanation: TypeScript improves reliability by checking types before runtime.
questions.prompt: Why is TypeScript designed for better developer efficiency?

questions.label: TS-03
questions.title: Transpilation Output

choices.text: It transpiles to plain JavaScript that runs anywhere JavaScript runs
It compiles to machine code only for Windows
It runs only in Deno and nowhere else

questions.explanation: TypeScript emits JavaScript, so it works in browsers and Node.js.
questions.prompt: What happens to TypeScript code before execution?

questions.label: TS-04
questions.title: Checking .js Files

choices.text: Yes, TypeScript can type-check plain .js files when enabled
No, TypeScript only checks .ts files and ignores .js completely
Only if Babel is used instead of tsc

questions.explanation: TypeScript supports checking JavaScript files with proper configuration.
questions.prompt: Can TypeScript check inconsistencies in JavaScript code?

questions.label: TS-05
questions.title: JS Type Checking Setup

choices.text: Use only ESLint and no TypeScript config

questions.explanation: JSDoc annotations plus checkJs enable static checks in .js files.
questions.prompt: Which combination enables type checking in JavaScript files?

questions.label: TS-06
questions.title: Gradual Typing
questions.explanation: TypeScript supports incremental adoption instead of full rewrites.
questions.prompt: What is a practical way to adopt TypeScript in an existing JS project?

questions.label: TS-07
questions.title: ECMAScript Output Version

choices.text: Set VS Code theme to dark mode

questions.explanation: Transpile output is controlled through compiler options in tsconfig.
questions.prompt: How do you control which ECMAScript version TypeScript emits?

questions.label: TS-08
questions.title: Target Option Location
questions.explanation: The target setting belongs under compilerOptions in tsconfig.
questions.prompt: Where is the TypeScript transpile target configured?

questions.label: TS-09
questions.title: Parse and Emit

choices.text: TypeScript parses code and emits JavaScript
TypeScript parses CSS and emits HTML
TypeScript parses SQL and emits JSON
TypeScript only lints files without output

questions.explanation: Compilation flow is parse/type-check/emit JavaScript.
questions.prompt: Complete the statement: TypeScript ___ your code and ___ JavaScript.

questions.label: TS-10
questions.title: What TypeScript Adds
questions.explanation: These are direct TypeScript productivity and safety benefits.
questions.prompt: Select all items listed as additions TypeScript brings to JavaScript.

questions.label: TS-11
questions.title: Typing Model

choices.text: JavaScript is dynamic; TypeScript is static
JavaScript is static; TypeScript is dynamic
Both are purely dynamic with no checks

questions.explanation: This is the core contrast highlighted in the notes.
questions.prompt: Which statement about JavaScript and TypeScript typing is correct?

questions.label: TS-12
questions.title: Compile-Time Checking

choices.text: Types are checked before running the code
Types are checked only after deployment
Types are checked only in the browser console

questions.explanation: TypeScript validates type consistency during compilation.
questions.prompt: What does compile-time type checking mean?

questions.label: TS-13
questions.title: TypeScript Drawbacks

choices.text: Cannot be used with JavaScript projects

questions.explanation: The notes list process overhead and discipline as tradeoffs.
questions.prompt: Select the drawbacks mentioned for TypeScript.

questions.label: TS-14
questions.title: Install TypeScript
questions.explanation: Global installation command in the notes uses npm -g.
questions.prompt: Which command installs TypeScript globally?

questions.label: TS-15
questions.title: Transpile a File
questions.explanation: The standard compiler command is `tsc <file>`.
questions.prompt: How do you transpile a TypeScript file to JavaScript?

questions.label: TS-16
questions.title: Initialize Config
questions.explanation: This generates a default tsconfig.json file.
questions.prompt: Which command creates a default `tsconfig.json`?

questions.label: TS-17
questions.title: Config File Location
questions.explanation: The notes explicitly say to place tsconfig.json at project root.
questions.prompt: Where should `tsconfig.json` be created?

questions.label: TS-18
questions.title: Important tsconfig Keys
questions.explanation: These are the exact key configs listed in the markdown.
questions.prompt: Select all configuration keys listed as important in the notes.

questions.label: TS-19
questions.title: removeComments

choices.text: Removes all code blocks before build

questions.explanation: This option controls comment emission in compiled JS output.
questions.prompt: What does `removeComments: true` do?

questions.label: TS-20
questions.title: noImplicitAny
questions.explanation: It raises errors when a value would be implicitly typed as any.
questions.prompt: What is the purpose of `noImplicitAny: true`?

questions.label: TS-21
questions.title: Unused Code Checks
questions.explanation: These options help keep code clean by catching unused declarations.
questions.prompt: What do `noUnusedParameters` and `noUnusedLocals` enforce?

questions.label: TS-22
questions.title: Unreachable Code Rule

choices.text: Disallows unreachable code when set to false
Enables dead code execution at runtime

questions.explanation: Setting `allowUnreachableCode` to false enforces stricter control flow.
questions.prompt: What is the effect of `allowUnreachableCode: false`?

questions.label: TS-23
questions.title: VS Code Debugging

choices.text: Add a breakpoint, open Debug panel, create launch.json, select Node.js
Only run npm install to debug
Use browser-only breakpoints for Node apps

questions.explanation: This sequence matches the debugging instructions exactly.
questions.prompt: Which workflow is described for debugging in VS Code?

questions.label: TS-24
questions.title: Launch Config Basics
questions.explanation: The provided launch config uses node launch mode.
questions.prompt: Select the correct `launch.json` values shown in the notes.

questions.label: TS-25
questions.title: Build Before Debug
questions.explanation: That exact value appears in `preLaunchTask`.
questions.prompt: What `preLaunchTask` value is used in the launch configuration?

questions.label: TS-26
questions.title: JavaScript Built-in Types
questions.explanation: These six were listed as JavaScript built-in types in the markdown.
questions.prompt: Select all JavaScript built-in types listed in the lesson.

questions.label: TS-27
questions.title: TypeScript Extended Types
questions.explanation: These are the TypeScript-extended types listed in the lesson.
questions.prompt: Select all TypeScript extended types from the notes.

questions.label: TS-28
questions.title: Explicit Typing

choices.text: You explicitly declare a variable's type
Types are assigned only at runtime

questions.explanation: Explicit typing means writing type annotations yourself.
questions.prompt: What does explicit typing mean in TypeScript?

questions.label: TS-29
questions.title: Type Inference

choices.text: TypeScript infers types from assigned values
Type inference works only for functions

questions.explanation: Inference reduces manual annotations in many cases.
questions.prompt: What is type inference in TypeScript?

questions.label: TS-30
questions.title: Tuple Concept

choices.text: A typed array with predefined length and index types
A dynamic object with unknown keys
A function that returns two values only

questions.explanation: Tuples are fixed-position, fixed-type arrays.
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes a TypeScript tuple?

questions.label: TS-31
questions.title: Tuple Safety Caveat

choices.text: Indexes 3+ may lose strict tuple safety when pushing new values
Tuples always reject push at compile time in all cases
Tuple values must all be strings

questions.explanation: The notes highlight this practical tuple limitation.
questions.prompt: What caution is shown in the tuple example?

questions.label: TS-32
questions.title: Enum Meaning

choices.text: An enum is a list of related constants
An enum is a runtime array method
An enum is a TypeScript package manager
An enum is a markdown syntax feature

questions.explanation: Enums group named constant values.
questions.prompt: What is an enum in TypeScript?

questions.label: TS-33
questions.title: const enum Benefit

choices.text: Compiler can generate more optimized code
It enables dynamic enum members at runtime

questions.explanation: The lesson states const enums help optimization.
questions.prompt: Why might you use `const enum`?

questions.label: TS-34
questions.title: Enum Naming Style
questions.explanation: The enum example explicitly notes PascalCase.
questions.prompt: What naming convention is shown for enums?

questions.label: TS-35
questions.title: Basic Type Concept
questions.prompt: What does the TypeScript `unknown` type require before usage?

choices.text: It can be used anywhere without checks
A type assertion or narrowing step

questions.explanation: Values typed as `unknown` must be narrowed or asserted before property access or assignment.

questions.label: TS-36
questions.title: Basic Typescript Concept
questions.prompt: Which compiler option enables strict null checking?
questions.explanation: `strictNullChecks` makes `null` and `undefined` distinct types unless explicitly included.

questions.label: TS-37
questions.title: Basic Typescript Concept
questions.prompt: What does `readonly` do on a TypeScript interface property?

choices.text: Removes the property from emitted JavaScript

questions.explanation: `readonly` prevents assignment after initialization at compile time.

questions.label: TS-38
questions.title: Basic Type Concept
questions.prompt: Which utility type makes all properties optional?
questions.explanation: `Partial<T>` maps every property in `T` to optional.

questions.label: TS-39
questions.title: Basic Type Concept
questions.prompt: What is the inferred type of `[1, 2, 3] as const`?
questions.explanation: `as const` freezes literal types and tuple readonlyness.
